title: Literal suffixes should be upper case
description: Enforces that literal suffixes (such as 'L', 'F', 'D') in Java code are written in upper case, improving code readability and reducing confusion. See the SonarSource documentation for details.
recommendation: Always use upper case for literal suffixes in numeric literals.
impact: Improves code readability and prevents subtle bugs due to misreading of literal types.

noncompliant:
long l = 1234l; // Noncompliant: lower case 'l' is hard to distinguish from '1'
float f = 1.0f; // Noncompliant: lower case 'f'
double d = 1.0d; // Noncompliant: lower case 'd'
compliant:
long l = 1234L; // Compliant
float f = 1.0F; // Compliant
double d = 1.0D; // Compliant

checks.message: Use uppercase literal suffixes (L, F, D) instead of lowercase for better readability.
